star_invariant.base_irrational: none (P7 and P5 are irreducible over Q; eigenvalues are degree 7 and 5 algebraic numbers)

path1_generalization.key_finding: R is geometry-specific; bridge width 1-2 degenerate
path1_generalization.result: POSITIVE — exact algebraic invariant family established

path2_random_sat.key_finding: No special behavior at alpha=4.267 (critical threshold)

path3_hodge_navier_stokes.key_finding: Valve = topological regularization; dual vertex-weakening / flow-strengthening
path3_hodge_navier_stokes.result: QUALITATIVE — analogy established, not quantitative bridge

proven_theorems: Thm 2.1: Grounded Laplacian as Schur Complement
Thm 3.1: Irreducibility of P_7 over Q
Thm 3.2: Irreducibility of P_5 over Q
Thm 8.2: Dual Effect of Valve Removal
open_conjectures: Conjecture 9.1: R < 2 for all star-cluster systems (bridge width >= 4)
Claim 7.1: Star as asymptotic limit of vortex stretching
Proposition 10.2: Spectral collapse bound prevents blow-up cascade

continuity_mapping.key_claim: Symmetric star graph is the asymptotic limit of vortex stretching events
continuity_mapping.implication: Spectral gap cannot halve under single valve operation; prevents enstrophy cascade
missing_pieces: Analytic proof of Conjecture 9.1 (R < 2 for all n, w >= 4)
Formalization via axisymmetric NS solutions (Hou-Li scenario)
Continuum limit of discrete spectral collapse sequence
